etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 恩(恩) (ēn) -> mouth sound to express acknowledgment
etymology.interpretation: The character 嗯(嗯) (èn, "mm-hmm; uh-huh") combines 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), indicating a relation to speech or sound, with 恩(恩) (ēn) which provides the phonetic component. This forms a character that represents a nonverbal vocalization used to express agreement or acknowledgment, often heard in conversations.

examples.chinese: 他问我是否明白，我说嗯(嗯)。
examples.pinyin: Tā wèn wǒ shìfǒu míngbái, wǒ shuō èn.
examples.english: He asked if I understood, and I said mm-hmm.

examples.chinese: 妈妈说完故事，我回应说嗯(嗯)，然后睡着了(然後睡著了)。
examples.pinyin: Māma shuō wán gùshì, wǒ huíyìng shuō èn, ránhòu shuìzháo le.
examples.english: After mom finished the story, I responded with uh-huh, and then fell asleep.

examples.chinese: 他讲笑话时(他講笑話時)，我们都说嗯(嗯)来表示赞同(來表示贊同)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jiǎng xiàohuà shí, wǒmen dōu shuō èn lái biǎoshì zàntóng.
examples.english: When he told the joke, we all said uh-huh to show agreement.
